Friend,

I'm the proud son of Vietnamese refugees, an Army veteran, and a small business owner. To be frank, I never expected to run for Congress.

I ran so our communities could be truly represented, and I don't take that responsibility lightly. It's why I’m reaching out with a few things you need to know as I gear up to defend CA-45 for the first time:
* **There are FIVE GOP challengers running against me** because national Republicans see this as one of their only opportunities to flip a Democratic seat and protect their House majority.

* **We flipped this seat by just 653 votes in 2024.** After Proposition 50 passed, it added tens of thousands of new voters to the district we need to reach with my message before my opponents.

* **The outside spending is reaching new heights.** The NRCC and national GOP groups are launching ads and online attacks earlier than before because they know this race could decide control of the House.

This is one of the toughest races in the country. If there was ever a moment to step up with a donation to boost our ability to win, this is it.

**[Can I count on you to chip in $3 or any amount before our mid-quarter deadline to help us stay competitive before the June 2nd primary?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131419929/621035820/1897980251?refcode=em-5.14.26-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDI3MThkMzItZjE0Zi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=9bcKlcCGy27grA2ovlwx7NN380HMSNlrwaZSZrO13w4=)**

We’re emailing to ask you to give right now to help retired Navy Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats.

And whether or not we defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat could decide control of the House. But right now, we’re still just 50 donations short of our daily goal and what we need to finally send Boebert packing.

name: Charles Booker
subject: The Mamdani of Kentucky
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
It’s time to flip this seat.
| Family,

You know the other side is getting nervous when they resort to silly name calling.

With just 5 days to go in this election, the Republican frontrunner for U.S. Senate in Kentucky called me “the Mamdani of Kentucky.” And then said I was a “radical socialist.” If it makes me a radical to believe every worker deserves a raise, so be it.

If it makes me a radical to believe we should protect workers in the face of massive AI disruption, so be it.

If it makes me a radical to believe we need a fair economy where all can have an affordable life, so be it.

**The truth is, I don’t think these are radical ideas. I think our government can and should work better for all of us.**

I think Mitch McConnell’s 40 year reign is ending and that we have an opportunity to choose a brighter path.

[Help me stand up to the mud slinging by Republicans in this race by making a split contribution to the Voter Protection Project and my campaign:](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109435?t=6&akid=24384%2E878179%2ECfDSJk) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Charles Booker and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109436?t=7&akid=24384%2E878179%2ECfDSJk)
---

[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/109435?t=11&akid=24384%2E878179%2ECfDSJk)
The latest polls have me way out in front but we need resources to reach every Kentucky voter by Election Day.

— Charles

name: KAREN CRNKOVICH FOR JOHNSON COUNTY COMMISSION
subject: Transparency should always come first...
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAREN CRNKOVICH FOR JOHNSON COUNTY COMMISSION * 6709 West 119th Street * Ste
body:
We need leaders who put transparency first.

| | | | | |
---
| |

Dear Peter,Leadership is not just about decisions.
It is about **how those decisions are made**.And in Johnson County, that matters now more than ever.When Mike Kelly became Commission Chair, one of his early decisions was to **remove public comments from the live stream**.Think about that.At a time when residents rely on access and transparency, the ability to hear directly from the public was taken away from those who could not attend in person. That decision has since been reversed. But it speaks to a broader pattern.Because this is not just about one decision. It is about a leadership style that too often operates behind the scenes and within a small circle. We have seen **work groups formed with limited commissioner participation** , including a housing work group with only three commissioners represented.When decisions are shaped in smaller, closed settings, it raises a simple question:**Who is at the table and who is not?** And more importantly:**Where is the voice of the taxpayer in those conversations?**
---
|
---
| |

Transparency is not selective.
It is not convenient.
And it is not something you turn on and off.It is a commitment to openness and accountability.I am running for Johnson County Commission Chair because I believe leadership should be different.It should be open to all commissioners.
Accessible to the public.
And accountable for every decision that impacts your tax dollars and your community.But changing that culture will not happen without a real effort.
---
| [DONATE TODAY](https://us.list-manage.com/yYr0qj8MAcB?e=7320325a61&c2id=7adf4106091b2bdf32986c5b36d3a494)
---
| |
Your support helps us reach voters, share what is really happening, and build a campaign strong enough to restore transparency and accountability in Johnson County.If you believe leadership should include people, not exclude them, I am asking you to stand with me. Together, we can bring openness and trust back to county government.With determination,
**Karen Crnkovich
** Candidate for Johnson County Commission Chair
---
| | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | [View email in browser](https://us20.campaign-archive.com/?e=7320325a61&u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=d50112e4a6)

In this email, I'm going to ask you to [**contribute $3**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131420267/621041947/1254288483?refcode=em-5.14.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWNjYzk5NzgtZjI0Zi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=IQ8Vob-s-JeI8ANx-S30VD3VswRJd6zj4hzVjdq-syY=) to keep our campaign running through the primary. We are weeks away from the election, so I hope you'll let me explain what's at stake. **Mail-in ballots are in homes across California for the June 2nd election. The means it's time to mobilize our voters in CA-30 – and to re-elect our first woman to represent this district in the House of Representatives.** The good news is that I'm confident in our message, and our grassroots team. During my many years in public service, I’ve been making tangible improvements to our region. With your help, we've made real progress on climate change, health care access, women's reproductive rights, and so much more. We are weeks away from the election, so I hope you'll let me explain what's at stake. Mail-in ballots are in homes across California for the June 2nd election. The means it's time to mobilize our voters in CA-30 – and to re-elect our first woman to represent this district in the House of Representatives. The good news is that I'm confident in our message, and our grassroots team. During my many years in public service, I’ve been making tangible improvements to our region. name: Nancy Lacore
subject: I’m concerned about Pete Hegseth.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
Hi there, it’s Nancy Lacore, the Democrat running to flip South Carolina’s First District.
I have a lot of thoughts about what’s been going on with Pete Hegseth.
Not only did he remove me from my position as Chief of the Navy Reserve after 35 years of service, but he’s actively undermining the foundation of our military.
Hegseth has fired more than a dozen senior military officers, including an Army chief of staff and two other generals who were ousted recently.
And now, he’s waging a war with Iran that could put our national security and countless American lives on the line.
That kind of behavior isn’t just destabilizing – it’s dangerous.
Now more than ever, we need leadership that’s rooted in honor, service, and respect. And that’s exactly why I’m running for Congress in South Carolina’s First District.
If we’re going to flip this seat and take back a Democratic House Majority, I need your help. Can you make a split donation of $25 or whatever you can, to Resist Reclaim Rebuild and my campaign? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
DONATE NOW TO HELP NANCY FLIP THIS SEAT! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
I’ll be honest, folks, I’m concerned.
While global tensions rise, the last thing we need is reckless leadership that’s willing to gamble with American lives.
We need steady, experienced leaders who are focused on delivering results – not inciting chaos, playing political games, and putting their own interests above the nation’s.
And I’m running for Congress to restore that kind of leadership – but we have a long road ahead of us to flip this seat and restore some accountability to Washington.

name: Josh Shapiro
subject: I have a lot on my mind
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAPIRO FOR PENNSYLVANIA]
body:
[ stdin# ]Josh Shapiro for Pennsylvania
Derek, I have a lot
on my mind right now.

I’ve been thinking about our primary election on May 19th, and the
consequences if our total vote tally falls well short of expectations.

I’ve been thinking about our fundraising slowdown, and the tough budget
decisions we’ll have to make if it continues through the general election.

I’ve been thinking about our opponent’s latest attack ad and the undecided
voters it could influence.

But Derek, I’ve
also been thinking about one more thing. Something just a little more
optimistic. I’ve been thinking about all the good things we can accomplish
— all the sh*t we can get done — by winning this election.

As long as we gather the resources to win this race, we can continue to
put more money in Pennsylvanians’ pockets. We can protect reproductive
freedoms, even as Donald Trump and his far-right enablers try to take them
away. We can continue to invest in our communities, our schools, our
people, and our economy — and build a Commonwealth where everyone has the
freedom to chart their own course and the opportunity to succeed.

[ https://act.joshshapiro.org/go/696?t=1001&akid=42%2E245147%2ERd2LQu ]Derek, all
of that progress is contingent on one thing, and one thing only: Winning
this election on November 3rd. And winning in November? It starts with
kicking off the general election in the strongest position possible.
Please, will you invest $3 right now to help me build that future?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

With gratitude,

Josh Shapiro

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football’s 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville’s time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year’s Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP’s SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul “Bear” Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville’s time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year’s Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP’s SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul “Bear” Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others. In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime. Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? PAID FOR BY TUBERVILLE FOR GOVERNOR, INC PO Box 43124 Birmingham, AL 35243 Copyright (C) 2026 Tuberville for Governor, Inc, All rights reserved.

name: Leah Donahey, LCV
subject: Big Oil is ripping us off at the pump. TAKE ACTION.
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTM3MDg3NjkS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjQ2MDYwMTcxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
Tell Congress:
Tax Big Oil Profits, Return Money to Everyday People!

Big Oil is using the war with Iran to profit off of everyday people who are
already struggling to make ends meet. Gas prices are out of control — up more
than 30% on average since the start of Trump’s current term. The American people need relief, and it should come from Big Oil’s profits.

Under the Big Oil Windfall Profits Tax Act, any extra profits billionaire oil
executives make by over-charging their customers would be returned to the public
instead of lining their already-deep pockets even further.

Tell Congress: tax Big Oil profits and return the funds to everyday people! https://links.messages.lcv. ADD YOUR NAME https://links.messages.lcv. Peter, Gas prices are soaring. Americans are broke. Gas companies are raking in record
profits. Trump's war in Iran is making everything worse. Here's how we fix it: To stop Big Oil and Gas from taking advantage of customers, Sen. Sheldon
Whitehouse (RI) and Rep. Ro Khanna (CA-17) introduced the Big Oil Windfall
Profits Tax Act. Under this bill, large oil companies would pay a tax on each
barrel of oil they produce or import above the average price in 2025. Revenue
from the tax would be returned to customers, who would receive about $216 to
$324 in rebates per year. Join LCV in calling on Congress to pass The Big Oil Windfall Profits Tax Act! https://links.messages.lcv. For more than a year, we’ve all been feeling the pain of Trump’s broken
promises. Energy costs were already unaffordable before the war began, and now working families’ wallets are taking
an even greater hit: The average price of a gallon of gas in the U.S. has increased by over 50% since Trump’s war in Iran began — jumping from $2.98 to $4.51. In 2025, Trump’s insistence on dirty oil, gas, and coal drove up electric bills by 13%. Now, with prices rising daily, Trump continues to block clean, affordable energy — like wind and solar — here at home. It’s time to usher in a new era of accountability for Trump and Big Oil’s
corporate greed. Take action today and urge Congress to pass the Big Oil Windfall Profits Tax Act. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/3Z0ec-8_ As long as the U.S. remains dependent on fossil fuels, working families will
keep paying the price — not only shrinking our wallets, but also our health,
safety, and future. The solution is building clean energy close to home,
creating power sources that lower costs, create jobs, and that are sustainable
for our communities and our climate. Thank you for taking action with LCV — for people and for our planet. Leah Donahey
Senior Federal Advocacy Campaigns Director
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=13708769&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjQ2MDYwMTcxOAS2&spReportId=MjQ2MDYwMTcxOAS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=13708769&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjQ2MDYwMTcxOAS2&spReportId=MjQ2MDYwMTcxOAS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.

Friend,

I promise I won't take up much of your time.

In my film _2000 Mules_ , I exposed one of the greatest scandals in American history - the hijacking of the 2020 presidential election.

Today, I'm proud to join forces with Stand in the Gap, a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting the patriots who exercised their First Amendment rights in protest of the _outcome_ of that election.

**I want to reach as many people as I can with this request...**

I told their founder, Shane Jenkins, that I would help him raise $20,000 before the end of the month to help Stand in the Gap provide help, hope, and healing to the January 6th patriots.

clean_body: Friend, I promise I won't take up much of your time. In my film 2000 Mules , I exposed one of the greatest scandals in American history - the hijacking of the 2020 presidential election. Today, I'm proud to join forces with Stand in the Gap, a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting the patriots who exercised their First Amendment rights in protest of the outcome of that election. I want to reach as many people as I can with this request... I told their founder, Shane Jenkins, that I would help him raise $20,000 before the end of the month to help Stand in the Gap provide help, hope, and healing to the January 6th patriots. Even a donation of $10 can give a January 6th patriot the support they need. Otherwise, let me explain why I 'm asking you to donate at least $10: $10 because the " crime" these patriots committed was fighting for their country. $10 because even though they've been pardoned, many of them are struggling to get back on their feet. $10 because Stand in the Gap is providing them with the resources they need to successfully reintegrate into their communities, find work, reconnect with family, and restore their rights. Not only are these men and women patriots, but they come from all walks of life -- law enforcement, veterans, white-collar professionals, blue-collar workers, and all races and religions. And they were hunted down in a political persecution that denied them their constitutional rights. Stand in the Gap exists to provide real, practical help, like rent money, job training, transportation, legal referrals, trauma counseling, and health insurance, to help these persecuted patriots move on with their lives. Your gift today can help one of these patriots find the help, hope, and healing they need to succeed in their second chance. Friend, can I count on you to contribute $10 or whatever you can afford to Stand in the Gap? Click any button below to give today. Thank you, Dinesh D'Souza P.S. If you're still reading this email and I haven't convinced you to donate $10 or more, please consider what January 6th patriots still face... Harassment on social media and in their personal lives Difficulties in finding a job Homelessness Loss of rights, like the right to vote or own a firearm * Mental health challenges that their families can never understand All they did was stand up for their beliefs. They do not deserve this sort of treatment. But for just $10, you can help Stand in the Gap give pardoned January 6th patriots the support they need to succeed at their second chance. Stand In The Gap is registered as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. All contributions are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. Stand In The Gap's tax identification number is 93-1417599. 5473 Blair Rd, Suite 100 PMB 917150 Dallas, TX 75231 | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Sarah McBride
subject: The stakes are high
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
With just six months left until Election Day, we shouldn’t put anything past Republicans.
# [](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1269?t=1&akid=3268%2E21716%2Ei6faOG)
With just six months left until Election Day, we shouldn’t put anything past Republicans. Because we’ve already seen that they will seek to suppress the vote and undermine the will of the people:
[](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260514-2.4togk8&refcode=em20260514-3268&t=2&akid=3268%2E21716%2Ei6faOG)
**That reinforces the need for us to win by a margin that’s too big to contest** — which means we need to reach voters who didn’t vote for us last time and compete in places we’ve written off in the past.
The stakes are high, and we’re going to need all the help that we can get. I’m working hard to defend Delaware’s seat in the House and support fellow Democrats who are running across the country, and I’m proud to rely on this grassroots team to help me do it.
Right now, my campaign is coming up on an important mid-month fundraising deadline. We need to raise $17,000 by midnight tomorrow night, and I need your help to reach it.
**So if you want to help me win re-election and flip the House this November, can I count on you to[make a donation of $5 or any amount right now to help us reach our mid-month goal and keep up the fight?](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260514-2.4togk8&refcode=em20260514-3268&amount=5&t=3&akid=3268%2E21716%2Ei6faOG)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20260514-2.4togk8&amount=5&refcode=em20260514-3268&t=22&akid=3268%2E21716%2Ei6faOG)**
Thank you,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-2026-footer?refcode=em20260514-2&t=23&refcode2=3268_21716_i6faOG&akid=3268%2E21716%2Ei6faOG)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

name: Justin J. Pearson
subject: A political lynching
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Committee to Elect Justin J
body:
| [ ](https://jj-p.org/l/jDR4usjT5Yrw?t=iNYY1OnT3uaP)
---
Peter, I'm emailing you more motivated than ever before.

**Republicans passed their racist, outrageous gerrymandered districts in Tennessee that eliminate District 9 as the only majority-Black and majority-Democratic district in our state.**

They have cheated their way into a map that favors Republicans, where politicians are picking their voters instead of voters picking their leaders.

We need to call this what it is: **this new map is a political lynching** — an affront to justice and the fight for equality.

My heart aches for my hometown of Memphis, where I was born and raised and where generations of my family are buried. [](https://jj-p.org/l/uCrwin9kMGmD) **But I refuse to give in to despair. Don't give up hope, Peter.** The fight still carries on, renewed with urgency as we work to restore the rights that our ancestors marched, prayed, bled, and died for.

[This righteous fight for our rights has never been more important, to push back against Jim Crow 2.0, to end the corruption that runs rampant and free, to give Black and Brown People a true voice in their democracy. Our fight continues: Will you please join me against authoritarianism and chip in $5 right away?](https://jj-p.org/l/YcUF9sjxfRil) | [CONTRIBUTE $5 »](https://jj-p.org/l/q7uMUMACdIFT)
---
The Supreme Court's ruling in _Louisiana v. Callais_ cleared the path for this, and I'm sure we're going to see the same playbook in other states soon, robbing Black and Brown People of their voting rights.

Last week, Republicans stole the only Black-majority district from us. But I want you to know: No matter what they do, no matter how much they try to break us and make us bend and quit — **we will still be here.**

I'm still going to fight to demonstrate strong leadership in TN, and I hope that you, as a member of this People-powered Movement, are still by my side.

This isn't over, and we will not stop until justice rolls down like waters and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream. [Will you chip in $5 or more in the next 30 minutes to fight this authoritarian power grab?](https://jj-p.org/l/hHCwnYvJCzPh)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $5 »](https://jj-p.org/l/TD2BRHBITQZB)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://jj-p.org/l/QcJmmGUEge2P)
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://jj-p.org/l/kKduPFTMDKAr)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://jj-p.org/l/EBed0lveKMGC)
[Or a custom donation »](https://jj-p.org/l/wC1g7llWBwfO)
In Love and Service,

Justin J. Pearson
| | Paid for by Committee to Elect Justin J. Pearson P.O. Box 9066, Memphis, TN 38190
www.votejustinj.com

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://jj-p.org/l/kk2vgBGU5PzL?t=YAxkBmAx54I3). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact, make a[monthly contribution by clicking here](https://jj-p.org/l/cQq5J0NVCTgD?t=FymDW0f2HJz7).**

[Click here](https://jj-p.org/fewer-emails?t=LhzBofQaCRf5) to receive fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://jj-p.org/l/i36JU3dR0Vmx). | | [Contribute Once Per Month to Elect Justin J. Pearson »](https://jj-p.org/l/llq04rJ1uOxc?t=cKNCCPxH5zpH)

name: Katie
subject: I can’t afford to ask twice.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc
body:
I’m not mincing words, Friend.
|
---
| | | | |
---
[](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)
|
---
It’s Senator Katie Britt, and I’m not mincing words: I can’t afford to ask twice, _Friend._
What I’m sharing matters, not just to me, but to _**everyone**_ counting on us to get this right.
**[I’m asking you, personally, to take a moment and read it:](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)**
|
---
[Read my note here >>](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)
|
---
\- Katie Britt
|
---
| | |
---
|
---
|
---
[](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)
I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?
|
---
[STAND WITH KATIE >>](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)
[](https://link.email.brittforsenate.)

name: Dr. Ami Bera
subject: A humble request
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
| Peter,

I have a humble request for you today. _[**Will you split a donation between my campaign and Dr. Jasmeet Bains’ campaign to help protect healthcare, flip CA-22, and take back the House majority?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131420415/621055527/1959058017?amount=15&source=EM_NBI_AB_20260514_FR_BainsSplit_0_6MO_DrAmiBera_NA_NA_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOWU2ODQxYy1mNjRmLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nSkWUpk1QIaYFJEaWDw1WnC-jnKwCt58sJM0miwjFM0=)_
**[SPLIT A DONATION>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131420416/621055528/1959058017?amount=15&source=EM_NBI_AB_20260514_FR_BainsSplit_0_6MO_DrAmiBera_NA_NA_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOWU2ODQxYy1mNjRmLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nSkWUpk1QIaYFJEaWDw1WnC-jnKwCt58sJM0miwjFM0=)**
Dr. Jasmeet Bains is a family doctor, daughter of immigrants, and the first Sikh American elected to the California Legislature. She’s spent her career caring for working families in California’s Central Valley and fighting to expand access to affordable healthcare.

Now, she’s running in one of the most competitive House races in the country against Republican David Valadao, **who recently voted for devastating Medicaid cuts that could impact 68% of the people in his district.** Her campaign was recently added to the DCCC’s Red-to-Blue program because Democrats know Californians are ready for leadership that puts families first, not MAGA political games.

**As a doctor myself, I know healthcare is not a political talking point. It’s personal. It’s about whether families can afford to see a doctor, fill a prescription, or get lifesaving treatment when they need it most.**

That’s why races like CA-22 matter so much. Democrats can flip this seat, but only if grassroots supporters step up early and help campaigns like Jasmeet’s build the resources needed to compete. And after the recent devastating Supreme Court decisions weakening voting rights protections, taking back the House majority runs through critical California races like hers and mine.

_[**Will you split a donation of $15 or anything you can today between my campaign and Dr. Jasmeet Bains to help us organize, reach voters, and win critical races in California?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/131420417/621055529/1959058017?amount=15&source=EM_NBI_AB_20260514_FR_BainsSplit_0_6MO_DrAmiBera_NA_NA_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOWU2ODQxYy1mNjRmLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nSkWUpk1QIaYFJEaWDw1WnC-jnKwCt58sJM0miwjFM0=)_

clean_body: | Peter, I have a humble request for you today. = = Dr. Jasmeet Bains is a family doctor, daughter of immigrants, and the first Sikh American elected to the California Legislature. She’s spent her career caring for working families in California’s Central Valley and fighting to expand access to affordable healthcare. Now, she’s running in one of the most competitive House races in the country against Republican David Valadao, who recently voted for devastating Medicaid cuts that could impact 68% of the people in his district. Her campaign was recently added to the DCCC’s Red-to-Blue program because Democrats know Californians are ready for leadership that puts families first, not MAGA political games. As a doctor myself, I know healthcare is not a political talking point. It’s personal. It’s about whether families can afford to see a doctor, fill a prescription, or get lifesaving treatment when they need it most. That’s why races like CA-22 matter so much. Democrats can flip this seat, but only if grassroots supporters step up early and help campaigns like Jasmeet’s build the resources needed to compete. And after the recent devastating Supreme Court decisions weakening voting rights protections, taking back the House majority runs through critical California races like hers and mine. = If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately by pressing a button below. = = = = = Thank you for standing with us, Dr. Ami Bera If you’d like to receive less email from our team If you'd like to stop receiving our emails, Paid for by Bera for Congress Bera for Congress PO Box 582496 Elk Grove, CA 95758 United States **

name: Team Jayapal
subject: Democracy needs you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
We’re coming up on our mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight tomorrow night.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260514-6237-footer&t=1&akid=6237%2E420207%2E1ZiqtF)
The clock is ticking, Peter!
We’re coming up on our mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight tomorrow night, and [**we need your help to reach our $17,000 goal**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-em-20260514.toLUsd&refcode=em20260514-6237&amount=3&t=2&akid=6237%2E420207%2E1ZiqtF) and keep this movement strong.
Right now, our democracy is facing attacks on every front.
ICE continues to terrorize immigrant communities with impunity, Donald Trump is waging a reckless and illegal war in Iran, and Republicans are gerrymandering maps to eliminate majority-Black districts and silence voters of color. All while prices continue to skyrocket — our President recently saying he “doesn’t think about Americans’ finances” at all.
**But we’re fighting back. We are growing our movement of nonviolent resistance. And we are progressing forward every single day.**
Pramila is on the frontlines in Congress demanding justice, defending our communities, and holding power to account. She doesn't answer to special interests — she answers to the people. Her work is sustained by small dollar donations from grassroots supporters like you, not corporations or billionaire donors.
[**To keep up our momentum and continue building this people-powered movement, we need to hit our $17,000 mid-month goal by midnight tomorrow night. Can you pitch in $3 right now to help us get there?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-em-20260514.toLUsd&refcode=em20260514-6237&amount=3&t=3&akid=6237%2E420207%2E1ZiqtF)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-em-20260514.toLUsd&amount=3&refcode=em20260514-6237&t=22&akid=6237%2E420207%2E1ZiqtF)**
Every contribution strengthens our grassroots movement and ensures Pramila has the resources to keep fighting in Congress. To keep fighting for our communities.
Thank you for showing up and standing with us.
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20260514-6237-footer&t=23&refcode2=6237_420207_1ZiqtF&akid=6237%2E420207%2E1ZiqtF)**

### Prevention, Innovation, and Impact Should Guide More Than Healthcare
| | [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)

| May 15

---

Mother's Day marked the beginning of **National Women 's Health Week (May 10-16, 2026)** -- a national initiative led by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services to encourage women to prioritize their physical, mental, and long-term health.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..vucwHe97Ak9_xeTpInIv7zc6QQFjt52OIs0RrPGfru8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=197601907)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/86009455-ec14-46df-b787-cc885bbeac30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

[National Women's Health Week](https://substack.com/redirect/87968324-9c65-443a-b7c4-c0c1d0f80ac9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This year's theme is:
**" Prevention, Innovation, and Impact: A New Era in Women's Health."**
[2026 National Women's Health Week Theme](https://substack.com/redirect/8ad06676-42c2-4c60-a43d-f735f8f1b4e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
It is an important message -- and one that extends far beyond healthcare alone.
In medicine, the best outcomes often come from early awareness, early detection, and early intervention. Preventive care reduces suffering, lowers long-term costs, improves quality of life, and strengthens families and communities.
_**That same mindset has value across nearly every area of public policy.**_
Too often, institutions and governments are _**reactive rather than proactive**_. Problems are addressed only after they become expensive, disruptive, or politically unavoidable. Whether the issue is healthcare access, chronic disease, mental health, infrastructure, affordability, education, or economic stability, the cost of waiting is usually far greater than the cost of prevention.
_**National Women 's Health Week serves as a reminder that prevention is not simply a medical concept -- it is a governing principle.**_
According to the Office on Women's Health, this year's initiative emphasizes:
* preventive screenings and early detection,
* chronic disease prevention,
* women-specific health research,
* innovation in care delivery,
* mental health,
* healthy aging,
* and improving long-term health outcomes for women across every stage of life.
[National Women's Health Week Focus Areas](https://substack.com/redirect/15130399-1c84-4cf8-8122-b1cc7d8c4b2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

_**These priorities matter because women often serve as the backbone of families, caregivers for multiple generations, leaders in their communities, and a critical part of the healthcare workforce itself.**_
At the same time, America faces growing healthcare pressures:
* rising costs,
* physician and nursing shortages,
* burnout among healthcare workers,
* increased chronic disease,
* mental health challenges,
* and growing frustration with systems that often feel administrative rather than patient-centered.

**The conversation around prevention is also backed by substantial economic data.**
According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, chronic diseases account for the vast majority of healthcare spending in the United States, with many conditions heavily influenced by preventable risk factors and delayed intervention.
[CDC Chronic Disease Overview](https://substack.com/redirect/026f8869-339f-4bfc-8f5e-76602ee0a970?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services estimates that U.S. healthcare spending continues to consume a significant share of the national economy and is increasing.
[CMS National Health Expenditure Data](https://substack.com/redirect/42504507-1ec9-4923-85bf-b859714818ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Prevention is not only compassionate policy. It is economically responsible policy.
And while National Women's Health Week focuses specifically on women's health, the broader lesson applies nationally: societies function better when they anticipate challenges rather than simply react to crises.
That includes:
* investing in preventive healthcare,
* strengthening maternal and mental health resources,
* supporting medical research,
* addressing workforce burnout,
* modernizing infrastructure before failures occur,
* improving environmental resilience,
* and focusing on long-term outcomes rather than short-term political cycles.

A healthier society requires long-term thinking.
_**This week is an opportunity to recognize the women who hold families and communities together while also embracing a broader national mindset rooted in prevention, innovation, preparation, and practical solutions.**_
_**Let 's continue honoring the women of America throughout National Women's Health Week -- and carry the spirit of prevention, innovation, and impact into every level of leadership and public policy.**_

clean_body: Prevention, Innovation, and Impact Should Guide More Than Healthcare | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more The Goldstein Substack - A Father-Son Publication Dr. Goldstein is Running for Congress and his page can be found Prevention, Innovation, and Impact Should Guide More Than Healthcare | | | May 15 | | | | | | | | | | | | Mother's Day marked the beginning of National Women 's Health Week (May 10-16, 2026) -- a national initiative led by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services to encourage women to prioritize their physical, mental, and long-term health. | | ---|---|--- This year's theme is: " Prevention, Innovation, and Impact: A New Era in Women's Health." It is an important message -- and one that extends far beyond healthcare alone. In medicine, the best outcomes often come from early awareness, early detection, and early intervention. Preventive care reduces suffering, lowers long-term costs, improves quality of life, and strengthens families and communities. That same mindset has value across nearly every area of public policy. Too often, institutions and governments are reactive rather than proactive. Problems are addressed only after they become expensive, disruptive, or politically unavoidable. Whether the issue is healthcare access, chronic disease, mental health, infrastructure, affordability, education, or economic stability, the cost of waiting is usually far greater than the cost of prevention. National Women 's Health Week serves as a reminder that prevention is not simply a medical concept -- it is a governing principle. According to the Office on Women's Health, this year's initiative emphasizes: preventive screenings and early detection, chronic disease prevention, women-specific health research, innovation in care delivery, mental health, healthy aging, and improving long-term health outcomes for women across every stage of life. These priorities matter because women often serve as the backbone of families, caregivers for multiple generations, leaders in their communities, and a critical part of the healthcare workforce itself. At the same time, America faces growing healthcare pressures: rising costs, physician and nursing shortages, burnout among healthcare workers, increased chronic disease, mental health challenges, and growing frustration with systems that often feel administrative rather than patient-centered. The conversation around prevention is also backed by substantial economic data. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, chronic diseases account for the vast majority of healthcare spending in the United States, with many conditions heavily influenced by preventable risk factors and delayed intervention. The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services estimates that U.S. healthcare spending continues to consume a significant share of the national economy and is increasing. Prevention is not only compassionate policy. It is economically responsible policy. And while National Women's Health Week focuses specifically on women's health, the broader lesson applies nationally: societies function better when they anticipate challenges rather than simply react to crises. That includes: investing in preventive healthcare, strengthening maternal and mental health resources, supporting medical research, addressing workforce burnout, modernizing infrastructure before failures occur, improving environmental resilience, and focusing on long-term outcomes rather than short-term political cycles. A healthier society requires long-term thinking. This week is an opportunity to recognize the women who hold families and communities together while also embracing a broader national mindset rooted in prevention, innovation, preparation, and practical solutions. Let 's continue honoring the women of America throughout National Women's Health Week -- and carry the spirit of prevention, innovation, and impact into every level of leadership and public policy.** The Goldstein Substack is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell The Goldstein Substack that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Michael Goldstein ? ?

body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240844%2E444211%2EnJnnsA)
You’re going to be getting a lot of political emails talking about “mid-month fundraising deadlines” complete with fundraising thermometers and desperate pleas for help.
It must work, or else it wouldn’t be so commonplace. But that’s not Powered by People.
Our goal is to send you emails you actually want to read that share stories from the road in Texas, highlight our incredible organizers and volunteers, and give you transparent and honest insights into our programming and fundraising.
Powered by People has 13 organizers and more than 1,000 volunteers across Texas.
We have a budget that dictates what we can spend on organizers, supporting volunteers, traveling — in other words, doing the work behind reaching, registering and turning out the voters we need to win political power in Texas.
Our average donation is just $23. This work is powered by people giving what they can — $5, $10, $23 at a time — because they believe Texas is worth fighting for.
[**If you’re able, please chip in $3 today to help Powered by People keep reaching, registering, and turning out the voters we need to elect Democrats in Texas this November and beyond.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260514.P6gTfk&refcode=ema20260514-240844&t=3&akid=240844%2E444211%2EnJnnsA)

Thank you,
Team PxP

name: Team Cooper (via Cooper Victory Fund)
subject: The honest truth
body:
The GOP already knows Roy has what it takes to flip NC’s seat.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6532?source=&t=1&akid=1394%2E124072%2EkGYp96)
| | | |
---
Peter,

With a $250,000 mid-month goal to reach by 11:59 p.m. tomorrow, is there any way we can persuade you to donate to Roy Cooper’s Senate campaign today?
We could tell you that Democrats have no chance at taking back the Senate majority without flipping North Carolina’s seat.
We could tell you that Roy Cooper has never lost a single election in North Carolina in his life.
We could also tell you that North Carolina hasn’t elected a Democrat for the U.S. Senate since 2008 – because all of the above is true.
That’s why, with almost $100 million in ad reservations already booked, the GOP is planning to spend more on North Carolina’s airwaves than in any other battleground state: **because they know that Senate control rides on this race and that Roy has what it takes to flip it blue.**
It’s also why we’ll only loosen the GOP’s grip on this seat in November with the grassroots support of thousands of folks like you from now until Election Day.
Ultimately, Peter, it’s your decision to make. All we can do ahead of tomorrow night’s deadline is ask: [**Will you please pitch in $5, or anything you can spare right now, to help us reach our $250,000 goal, power Roy Cooper to victory in North Carolina and flip the Senate blue?**](https://act.roycooper.com/go/11287?amount=5&t=8&akid=1394%2E124072%2EkGYp96)
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/11285?amount=5&t=9&akid=1394%2E124072%2EkGYp96)

---

---
Team Cooper
| |
* * *
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8188?t=2&akid=1394%2E124072%2EkGYp96)
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**_[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6533?source=&t=3&akid=1394%2E124072%2EkGYp96)_**

name: Amanda for Colorado via ActionNetwork.org
subject: They told me no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda for Colorado
body:
| | | Hi Peter, People in Colorado's jails who aren’t serving time for felony convictions, those who haven't been convicted of anything, or are serving time for misdemeanors, still have the right to vote. For years, very few of them did. The year I started asking questions,**just three (out of several hundred) eligible voters actually voted** from the Jefferson County jail**.** I asked to bring in-person voting into my county's jail. **They told me no.** **So** **I helped pass a law requiring it.** Turnout in my county's jail went from **less than 1% to over 40% in a single election.** One of the first people to vote from jail after the law changed told me it was one of the top experiences of his life. **He said it made him feel like he mattered.** That's why I do this work. And that's why I'm running for Secretary of State—to make sure your voice is heard and your vote is counted. We’re in the homestretch and I’m making every dollar count. Can you help us reach more voters before ballots arrive? [DONATE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu9tkS213ThEvbRM6r3opeYuU4W04YpDvXwBZQsTw3tVCcdBOOgK0I7HlDqdZWdshFz4ubFO9ae68JfH5LfeHxZ4ufaBeeKQm2k5EBC8RP6x0Khv7Q7EXrQ6BId9ZtqlLyWKrULlK-4DXtbhL9MGawmPYuFMBR2g4Hiufi6aik9GNClaUAhOeo4fKY2CeqlgaOnTtES6wwT9WBSG4qzRqg3nxQG7jelScZXWZ3kprUN2dzOhfwmWHxaqEwoyGgN8kw/4qn/Rq1b39l2Sa6CpBxvd2h3xQ/h0/h001.BBvXLFIgn_GVGmEAo4CzZkeKeFTn4MMddPOm7imUUGk)
This is our democracy. We're taking it back. Amanda Gonzalez Candidate for Colorado Secretary of State _
_ _Paid for by Amanda for Colorado. Registered Agent Ashton Narzisi._ _
_

**TOP NEWS**
**" Corpus Christi moves toward mandatory 25% cut in water use if emergency is declared"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/89da882e-3e25-4bba-8502-bc21fe846b24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Alex Nguyen** and **Colleen DeGuzman** - Corpus Christi residents and businesses -- including one of the nation's largest petrochemical corridors -- may be required to cut water use by 25% during a water emergency that city leaders expect to hit by September unless a devastating drought eases.

The City Council voted 7-2 Tuesday to give initial approval to a water curtailment plan that would set water limits for different customers, ranging from residents and local businesses to large oil companies. The council is expected to take a final vote adopting the curtailment plan on May 19.

City leaders for months have been bracing for a Level 1 water emergency -- the point when the city's supply is projected to be 180 days away from falling short of demand. If there's no significant rainfall soon, experts are predicting that may be around four months away. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/89da882e-3e25-4bba-8502-bc21fe846b24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Report: Texas public schools have largest non-Covid enrollment drop in recorded history"** via [KXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/30f9ba0a-1dad-4d8b-a3d4-df554846c131?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Adam Schwager** - Total public school enrollment in Texas dropped by over 76,000 students, according to a Texas 2036 report released on Monday morning. The 2025-26 school year is only the second time public school education has dropped in recorded Texas history, dating back to the 1987-88 school year. The other recorded drop happened in the 2020-21 school year, during the height of the COVID-19 shutdowns.

The enrollment drop is happening across the state, with only the Abilene area seeing an increase in enrollment, and only certain areas in central and east Texas holding steady. The biggest drops are taking place in south and west Texas, with the Midland, Amarillo, Edinburg, and San Angelo regions all losing at least 2.5% of their student population.

"(The decline is) spread out across all types of students, it's spread out across all areas of the state," **Mary Lynn Pruneda** , the Director of Education and Workforce Policy for Texas 2036, said. "So that makes it really hard to point to one particular thing and say, 'Yes, this is definitely the reason that there are fewer kids in public schools today.'

Schools are also disproportionately losing Hispanic and White students. While the overall public school population dropped 1.4%, the Hispanic population dropped by 2.1% and the White population dropped by 1.9%. Overall, Hispanic students made up 81% of the loss enrollment loss. The data also shows declines in traditionally underserved communities -- with homeless students dropping by 8% and economically disadvantaged students dropping by 2.3%. _([KXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/30f9ba0a-1dad-4d8b-a3d4-df554846c131?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" CBP axes plan to build portion of border wall in Big Bend National Park"** via [Spectrum News](https://substack.com/redirect/f26bd839-73d6-4e31-8be3-fe83ee32c8a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- A top U.S. Customs and Border Patrol (CBP) official told the Washington Examiner that plans to build a 30-foot-tall border wall in Big Bend National Park have been axed.

CBP Commissioner **Rodney Scott** told the Examiner it would be "kind of silly" to put a border wall on top of the park's towering granite cliffs.

Instead of the physical barrier, Scott said officials will pave roads along the border of the national park and utilize drones and other digital surveillance equipment.

"So what we're trying to convey is that we are going to have meaningful border security in that entire area," Scott said. _([Spectrum News](https://substack.com/redirect/f26bd839-73d6-4e31-8be3-fe83ee32c8a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Excessive heat suspected as cause of death after six bodies found in rail car near Laredo"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/d32d4294-ad95-4819-9137-bb2a042defaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Berenice Garcia** and **Alex Nguyen** - Extreme heat is suspected to have played a role in the deaths of six people from Mexico and Honduras whose bodies were discovered inside a train car in Laredo.

A 29-year-old woman from Mexico and a 24-year-old man from Honduras were among the six deceased individuals found inside a shipping container at Union Pacific Railyard on Sunday afternoon, according to Webb County officials. The other four individuals were all male and included one teenager, though their identities had not yet been confirmed.

The Webb County Medical Examiner determined that the woman died due to hyperthermia, or overheating. Hyperthermia is also suspected to have caused the death of the other five individuals, though formal examinations for them are still pending, according to a news release issued by the county.

**Corinne Stern** , the county's medical examiner, found identification cards and cellphones that indicated the individuals were from Mexico and Honduras, according to the Associated Press. Their fingerprints were also shared with the U.S. Border Patrol to help confirm their identities and nationalities through the Missing Alien Program. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/d32d4294-ad95-4819-9137-bb2a042defaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Report reveals how much water could be consumed by Texas data centers"** via [Houston Chronicle](https://substack.com/redirect/0873c39b-91d2-49e1-abb1-3c0383b02bbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Michael Garcia** - Texas' booming data center industry could strain the state's already stressed water supply, with facilities projected to account for up to 9% of statewide water use by 2040, according to a University of Texas study.

The report, which accounted for water used for cooling and to produce the needed power at data centers, comes as many data centers are planned throughout the state. With researchers at UT saying there are more than 400 data centers operating or under construction in Texas, they're urging for more transparency, improved planning and alternative water sources to reduce pressure on supplies, the study states.

Currently, data centers account for less than 1% of the state's water use.

"There's a lot of uncertainty surrounding the water use for data centers," said **Mariam Arzumanyan** , a fellow at the Bureau of Economic Geology at UT Austin's Jackson School of Geosciences, in a written statement. "People don't know the scale of how much water is going to be needed. There is not a unified understanding of the technologies used for cooling, or how much water that technology would use." _([HOU CHRON](https://substack.com/redirect/0873c39b-91d2-49e1-abb1-3c0383b02bbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Gas power leapfrogs wind for first time in 10 years in Texas' grid connection queue"** via [San Antonio Report](https://substack.com/redirect/e96abab3-608d-4a8b-9a0b-ff3969c3b89d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Brandon Mulder** - A decade ago, wind power was surging in popularity and attracting huge investments that made Texas a national leader in renewable energy. But today, gas generation is making a big comeback, driven by a wave of data centers flooding into the state.

For the last six months, the volume of gas generation in the Texas grid's interconnection queue -- the yearslong waiting list for electric generators wanting to connect to the grid -- has surpassed wind. It's the first time since January 2016 that gas has overtaken wind in the queue, a shift that reflects the policy and economic headwinds facing the wind industry and data centers favoring gas power as they seek to cash in on the artificial intelligence boom.

"The data center explosion and their desire for 24/7 power probably excited a lot of gas developers, and that gas queue got bigger," said University of Texas professor of energy regulation **David Spence**.

Like every power grid operator in the U.S., the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, or ERCOT, uses the interconnection queue to manage and plan for new power generation coming online. But not all projects in the queue ultimately reach completion. According to the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, or LBNL, only 22% of projects in ERCOT's queue actually get built, which is still the highest percentage of any grid in the nation. _([SA REPORT](https://substack.com/redirect/e96abab3-608d-4a8b-9a0b-ff3969c3b89d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Houston's fastest-growing suburb is quietly sinking"** via [Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/98798664-f4f0-4281-82dc-52bca38a4e3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Molly Wilhelm** \-- Katy--located west of Houston and long-ranked among the state's fastest-expanding communities--is sinking faster than any other suburb in the Houston area, according to a recent report from the Houston-Galveston Subsidence District.

Over roughly the last two decades, Katy has sunk approximately 14 inches, according to the report.

Between 2021 and 2025, the Houston-Galveston Subsidence District recorded an average annual subsidence rate of about one inch in Katy. The organization defines subsidence as the sinking of the Earth's surface due to subsurface movement.

"There is still ongoing subsidence, particularly in western Harris County in the Katy and Fulshear areas, with some rates showing over two centimeters per year," **Ashley Greuter** , the district's director of research and water conservation, said during a presentation of the report in late April. _([Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/98798664-f4f0-4281-82dc-52bca38a4e3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" State of Texas: Poll shows Cornyn, Paxton in tight runoff race for Republican U.S. Senate nomination"** via [CW39 Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/94b7b1d1-519d-4a04-bcdc-e804da5fe72b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Adam Schwager** - Voters are just a few weeks away from a runoff election that could signal a major shakeup in Texas politics. The runoff will determine whether incumbent U.S. Senator **John Cornyn** will get the chance to defend his seat in the November election. New polling shows a tight race between Cornyn and Attorney General **Ken Paxton**.

The Hobby School of Public Affairs at the University of Houston surveyed 1,200 likely Republican runoff voters between April 28 and May 1. The results show Paxton with 48% support and 45% support for Cornyn. The margin of error for the poll is +/- 2.83%.

**Mark P. Jones** , political science fellow at Rice University's Baker Institute for Public Policy and a senior research fellow at the Hobby School, noted that Paxton picked up a notably larger share of the support from voters who cast a ballot in March for third-place finisher **Wesley Hunt** , with 53% of Hunt voters backing Paxton and 34% opting for Cornyn in the runoff.

With both candidates running neck-and-neck, an endorsement from President Trump could change the entire race. "Trump set a precedent with his endorsement of (Republican Comptroller nominee) **Don Huffines** and (incumbent Agriculture Commissioner) **Sid Miller** back in March, where he was willing to wait until the last day of early voting... until dawn on Election Day, May 26, we can't actually rule out a **Donald Trump** endorsement," Jones said. _([CW39 Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/94b7b1d1-519d-4a04-bcdc-e804da5fe72b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Former President Obama makes surprise stop in Austin with James Talarico, Gina Hinojosa"** via [Austin American-Statesman](https://substack.com/redirect/7dd696dd-a69e-456b-9892-9510b1298857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **John C. Moritz** - Former Presiden**t Barack Obama** appeared alongside U.S. Senate candidate**James Talarico** and gubernatorial candidate **Gina Hinojosa** on Tuesday in Austin as part of an attempt to boost a new crop of Democratic candidates ahead of this year's midterms.

The appearance was not a formal endorsement, but Obama introduced the two to a crowd of awestruck lunch goers at a taco restaurant east of the University of Texas as "the next senator and governor of Texas." Democrats haven't won a statewide contest in Texas in 32 years.

"Now everybody remember to vote," the former president said as the young crowd jockeyed for photos and pleaded for autographs. _([AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/7dd696dd-a69e-456b-9892-9510b1298857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Immigration a flashpoint in Allred-Johnson Democratic runoff"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/d8556a19-8dfe-4e8b-839d-95c325c8f02d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Olivia Borgula** -- As he campaigned for the Senate in 2024, then-U.S. Rep. **Colin Allred** sought to appeal to a wide range of voters, many of whom were dissatisfied with the Biden administration's border policies.

He ran an ad standing side by side with law enforcement that touted him as "tough" and "standing up to extremists in both parties." He broke from Democrats on multiple immigration bills, including the **Laken Riley** Act, which required no-bond detention for undocumented immigrants accused of certain crimes, including lower-level offenses like shoplifting.

Two years later, Allred is running for Congress again, but this time in a solidly blue, Dallas-centered seat. He's now calling to abolish Immigration and Customs Enforcement and denouncing for-profit immigration enforcement -- positions that prompted his primary runoff opponent, Rep. **Julie Johnson** , to label him a "flip-flopper."

Allred's changed campaign focus is emblematic of a broader shift among Democrats in a vastly different political climate under President **Donald Trump**. His administration's immigration raids and mass deportations have sparked outrage among many Democrats who spent the Biden era playing defense on immigration. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/d8556a19-8dfe-4e8b-839d-95c325c8f02d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Christian Menefee, Alex Mealer lead Houston-area congressional runoffs, new poll finds"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/974833eb-1271-4d8d-8dfa-44024db3c17a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Gabby Birenbaum** -- Rep.**Christian Menefee l** eads Rep.**Al Green** by 7 percentage points heading into the final days of the runoff, according to a new poll of the closely watched race between two Houston Democratic members of Congress.

The University of Houston's Hobby School of Public Affairs polled likely voters in two Houston-area contests -- the heavily Democratic 18th Congressional District, where Green and Menefee are running, and the red-leaning 9th Congressional District's Republican runoff between state Rep. **Briscoe Cain** , R-Deer Park, and Army veteran **Alex Mealer**.

In the 18th District, the poll found Menefee garnering 50% of the vote to Green's 43%. The poll was conducted from May 5 to 8 and included a sample of 800 likely voters. It has a margin of error of +/-3.46 percentage points.

The two are running against one another after Texas GOP legislators redrew the state's congressional map last summer, moving the 9th District, which Green has represented for over 20 years, to new territory that favors the GOP, and in the process putting a large share of Green's current constituents into the new 18th District. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/974833eb-1271-4d8d-8dfa-44024db3c17a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Harris County Treasurer Carla Wyatt's latest arrest adds to growing legal troubles during re-election campaign"** via [Click2Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/45a138d4-c162-45a4-8d44-ff8861ec2c95?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Jaewon Jung** -- Harris County Treasurer Dr. **Carla Wyatt** is facing legal trouble again after being arrested for driving while intoxicated in Galveston County over the weekend.

According to Galveston County jail records, Wyatt was arrested Saturday on a DWI charge and later released on a $3,000 bond. KPRC 2 has learned she was arrested in Texas City.

The arrest marks Wyatt's second DWI arrest and her third arrest since taking office as Harris County Treasurer.

Court records show Wyatt was previously arrested for DWI in Houston in December 2023. _([Click2Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/45a138d4-c162-45a4-8d44-ff8861ec2c95?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Texas truckers struggle after noncitizen licenses canceled"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/73b185f1-cd7d-4e39-8640-279ef55be918?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Colleen DeGuzman** and **Alex Nguyen** - **Veronica Viera** loved watching Texas unfold from the driver's seat of her bright pink 18-wheeler -- the winding roads, the small towns, the sense of freedom -- so losing her license in a government crackdown on legal immigrants was devastating.

No more trips to and from Houston, listening to pop and Christian music. No more hauling whiskey from Laredo, tangerines from Brownsville, solar panels to the Hill Country.

Viera's life abruptly changed when Texas, following the dictates of the Trump administration, canceled commercial driver's licenses, or CDLs, held by legally present noncitizens, including asylum seekers, refugees and DACA recipients. Texas was one of the first states to take action.

She heard about the cancellations in December as men unloaded office chairs she'd hauled to San Antonio in the beloved semi she named Pink Panther. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/73b185f1-cd7d-4e39-8640-279ef55be918?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Could Texas eliminate school property taxes? What to know"** via [San Antonio Express-News](https://substack.com/redirect/3daa8248-3d56-454b-b90d-8aed97030c4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Scott Huddleston** -**** With deadlines quickly approaching for Texans to protest their property value appraisals, school districts are starting to forecast how changing values will affect their budgets.

Texas schools are funded by two main pots of money. The state determines how much funding it takes to educate each district's students, accounting for special student needs like dyslexia services or career and technical education courses.

Each year, school districts levy a local property tax to raise money to pay for their budgets. Some districts with higher property values are able to fund their entire budget out of this local tax revenue. But districts with lower property tax values may still need more support to fill their coffers. That's where the state comes in -- kicking in funding to make districts whole. _([SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/3daa8248-3d56-454b-b90d-8aed97030c4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Fort Worth to review plans for $10 billion data center development"** via [Fort Worth Star-Telegram](https://substack.com/redirect/856b2c79-50e7-402d-9782-1709bd598f9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Emily Holshouser** - The Fort Worth City Council is expected to review the site plan for a data center Tuesday, May 12, planned for the city's southeast edge that has brought concern to residents and some leaders.

Black Mountain, a Fort Worth-based energy consortium, has hit roadblocks in the development of an AI data center project after successfully petitioning the city to rezone roughly 431 acres near Forest Hill and Everman.

On Tuesday, the council will review a 187-acre site plan for the development that the zoning commission recommended approval for in April. The site, at the corner of Lon Stephenson Road and Forest Hill Drive, was initially rezoned in 2025.

The site plan includes a 70-foot increase in the setback against Lon Stephenson Road, putting the edge of the campus 150 feet from single-family residential zoning, and an increase in the allowed height of the buildings on the site from 55 feet to 70 feet. _([FWST](https://substack.com/redirect/856b2c79-50e7-402d-9782-1709bd598f9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Austin leaders push for faster action on troubled 911 system"** via [Austin American-Statesman](https://substack.com/redirect/df1fb1f8-483a-4d58-ad71-7c710f14db9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Austin Sanders** - The 911 Communications Center at the Austin Police Department on Thursday, September 14, 2023.

Austin City Council members pressed public safety officials Tuesday for answers on why the city is months behind on a plan to consolidate emergency communications, warning that the delays leave residents in some parts of the city with less reliable 911 services.

At a special-called meeting of the City Council's Public Safety Committee, officials provided an update on the city's progress on an initiative to integrate 911 call-taking and dispatch functions into one centralized department, but **Krista Laine** and other council members balked at the lack of progress on a project ordered in August.

Laine, who introduced the original resolution calling for the consolidated emergency communications center, said Tuesday's presentation was delivered months late and fell far short of what City Council requested. _([AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/df1fb1f8-483a-4d58-ad71-7c710f14db9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Texas school districts face first deadline from law requiring seat belts on buses"** via [CBS News](https://substack.com/redirect/a3224af0-a8cf-4436-9843-68ffb788ddfa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s**Lacey Beasley** - The first deadline for a new Texas law on school bus safety is approaching.

Senate Bill 546, which passed during the 2025 regular legislative session, requires all public school buses to have three-point seat belts by 2029. Three-point seat belts include a shoulder belt and a lap belt, as opposed to a two-point belt, which only goes across the lap.

While the deadline to install the belts is still years away, districts must report to the TEA how much it will cost them to retrofit or replace their buses by May 29. That includes buses operated by or contracted for use by the school district.

The Legislature did not provide funding for the mandate; school boards may either comply by using district funds, or they may approve an exemption if funding is not available. _([CBS News](https://substack.com/redirect/a3224af0-a8cf-4436-9843-68ffb788ddfa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar faces removal attempt following federal fraud indictment"** via [The Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/c71fdaca-b8cd-453c-bba6-812a96a9f301?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Alex Nguyen** - Nearly six months after being indicted on federal fraud charges, Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar is now facing an attempt to remove him from office.

Former Laredo City Council member **Alfonso "Poncho" Casso** filed the petition to remove Cuellar in Webb County district court last week, citing the federal indictment and other misconduct allegations. Casso's filing, which was first reported by The Laredo Morning Times, asked the court to suspend the five-term South Texas sheriff from office pending a trial that seeks to permanently remove him from the post.

Cuellar denied all allegations outlined in the petition and "demands strict proof," according to a response filed in court on Friday. He already pleaded not guilty to the federal charges, which were unsealed in January.

Casso's petition largely relies on the five-count indictment, which accuses Cuellar and two other people of misappropriating funds from the Webb County sheriff's office during the COVID-19 pandemic. _([TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/c71fdaca-b8cd-453c-bba6-812a96a9f301?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_

**" Texas city ousts top attorney amid backlash over local election changes"** via [MySA](https://substack.com/redirect/a2d0f4e8-a10b-4c22-9f33-dbe653d04510?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s **Nicholas Hernandez** - New Braunfels officials voted Monday night to remove City Attorney Valeria Acevedo after a week of fallout tied to the city's mayoral election, creating uncertainty around the city's leadership just weeks before a scheduled runoff.

In a 4-3 decision, the City Council sided with Mayor Pro Tem **Lawrence Spradley** and councilmembers **Michael Capizzi** , **Lee Edwards** and Mayor **Neal Linnartz** to terminate Acevedo. Councilmembers **Toni Carter** , **Mary Ann Labowski** and **April Ryan** voted against the move.

The decision follows confusion surrounding the May 2 mayoral race, where early results showed challenger **Michael French** leading with less than 50% of the vote. While the city charter appeared to allow a candidate to win by plurality, officials later determined that state law requires a majority in races with terms longer than two years, forcing a runoff.

That reversal, which came days after initial results were announced, drew criticism from residents and scrutiny over how the inconsistency went unnoticed. The issue was ultimately flagged by outside legal counsel, prompting the city to reassess the election outcome before it was finalized. _([MySA](https://substack.com/redirect/a2d0f4e8-a10b-4c22-9f33-dbe653d04510?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))_
